title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in model safeguards and evaluation: a new system card and bio red-teaming program from OpenAI, plus fresh research on multi-turn jailbreaks, memorization, and evaluation design. The common thread is that safety claims increasingly hinge on how models are tested across realistic interaction patterns, sensitive domains, and deployment-specific failure modes.

items.title: OpenAI publishes GPT-5.5 system card
items.why_it_matters: System cards are primary evidence for model risk assessment, safeguards, and deployment constraints.

items.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5 bio bug bounty
items.why_it_matters: It expands external red teaming for high-consequence biological misuse and jailbreak resilience.

items.title: Transient Turn Injection exposes stateless multi-turn LLM vulnerabilities
items.why_it_matters: It highlights how turn-by-turn moderation can miss attacks distributed across a conversation.

items.title: Revisiting non-verbatim memorization in LLMs examines entity surface forms
items.why_it_matters: It suggests memorization audits may understate privacy and leakage risks under varied prompts.

items.title: CrossCommitVuln-Bench targets vulnerabilities invisible to per-commit static analysis
items.why_it_matters: It offers a benchmark for evaluating security tools on realistic, temporally distributed software flaws.

items.title: Fine-tuning regimes define distinct continual learning problems
items.why_it_matters: It argues evaluation results can shift materially with training setup, complicating capability and safety comparisons.

items.title: Temporal taskification in streaming continual learning is flagged as an evaluation instability
items.why_it_matters: It identifies benchmark construction choices as a source of unstable or misleading model assessments.

items.title: OpenAI releases Privacy Filter for PII detection and redaction
items.why_it_matters: Privacy tooling can reduce downstream exposure of sensitive personal data in AI workflows.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
